# Tests list-template-callers with multiple --use-templates-variable flags.
# Each variable's callers are reported in its own section, with no duplication
# across variables. admin-layout is only referenced from the adminTemplates
# variable and public-layout only from publicTemplates, so each appears once
# in the combined output.

muxt list-template-callers --use-templates-variable=adminTemplates --use-templates-variable=publicTemplates

cp stdout out.txt
count-matches 'template "admin-layout" called by:' out.txt 1
count-matches 'template "public-layout" called by:' out.txt 1
count-matches 'template "header" called by:' out.txt 2

# JSON output across multiple variables must be a single valid JSON document,
# not concatenated objects.
muxt list-template-callers --use-templates-variable=adminTemplates --use-templates-variable=publicTemplates --format=json

cp stdout out.json
count-matches '^\{' out.json 1

-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.22
-- template.go --
package server

import (
	"embed"
	"html/template"
	"io"
)

//go:embed admin/*.gohtml
var adminFS embed.FS

//go:embed public/*.gohtml
var publicFS embed.FS

var adminTemplates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(adminFS, "admin/*.gohtml"))
var publicTemplates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(publicFS, "public/*.gohtml"))

type T struct{}

func init() {
	_ = adminTemplates.ExecuteTemplate(io.Discard, "admin-layout", T{})
	_ = publicTemplates.ExecuteTemplate(io.Discard, "public-layout", T{})
}
-- admin/layout.gohtml --
{{define "admin-layout"}}{{template "header" .}}{{end}}
{{define "header"}}<header>admin</header>{{end}}
{{define "footer"}}<footer>admin</footer>{{end}}
-- public/layout.gohtml --
{{define "public-layout"}}{{template "header" .}}{{end}}
{{define "header"}}<header>public</header>{{end}}
{{define "footer"}}<footer>public</footer>{{end}}
